title: Cyclosorus ciliatus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus ciliatus is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. The genus Cyclosorus belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae and is a type of fern. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and its classification as a fern reflects its vascular structure and leaf morphology. The species is further classified into the family Thelypteridaceae, subfamily Thelypterioideae, and tribe Thelypterideae. The classification of Cyclosorus ciliatus is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Cyclosorus ciliatus has been found to have various medicinal properties, primarily used in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever and other health issues. The leaves of the plant are often used to treat digestive problems and as a diuretic. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of certain infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Cyclosorus ciliatus.

intro: Cyclosorus ciliatus, also known as the ciliate lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. The ciliate lip fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus ciliatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, teas, and extracts, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
